postData.contentHtml:
The Indiana Fever faced an unexpected setback on Thursday as key guard Sophie Cunningham was ruled out of their matchup against the Chicago Sky. Cunningham, known for her valuable contributions off the bench, is nursing a right elbow injury – notably, her shooting arm.

Initially listed as questionable, the decision to sideline Cunningham came just hours before tip-off. While the exact severity and recovery timeline remain unclear, her initial 'questionable' status offers a glimmer of hope that this may not be a long-term absence. With the Fever's next game scheduled for Saturday, a rapid turnaround could be on the horizon if the injury proves minor.

Cunningham's absence leaves a significant gap in the Fever's rotation. Averaging 8.7 points per game this season, her offensive spark and defensive versatility will be missed. In her stead, Lexie Hull is expected to see increased playing time, and the team will likely need to dig deeper into its bench to compensate for Cunningham's absence. The team's ability to adapt and maintain performance without one of its vital role players will be a key storyline in the coming days.

Social media reaction to the news has been largely positive, albeit with some debate. Fans are discussing the impact on the team's performance and speculating on her potential return, highlighting her importance to the Fever's dynamic.
postData.title: Fever Hit with Injury Blow: Sophie Cunningham Sidelined with Elbow Ailment
postData.summary: Indiana Fever guard Sophie Cunningham was a late scratch for Thursday's game against the Chicago Sky due to a right elbow injury, raising questions about the team's depth and her quick return.
